review_question: Should required-bucket pass/fail remain moneyness-only, or should a pre-registered side-aware or notional-weighted coverage policy be evaluated for H-G1 diagnostics?

candidate_policies.description: Keep current v2 moneyness-only required-bucket row-rate and notional gates.
Use side-aware pass/fail for otm_put and otm_call while reporting opposite-right ITM rows separately.
Keep moneyness bucket labels but treat row-rate failures as warnings when OI-notional and gamma-proxy representation pass.

acceptance_criteria: Validator passes before any comparison rerun.
All candidate policies use manifest-v3 rows only.
Opposite-right ITM rows are reported explicitly.
Timestamp discipline and raw-row coverage remain visible.
Any selected candidate is justified by mechanism and data lineage, not by strategy PnL.
Result remains E1 or lower until a separate strategy-independent validation report exists.
failure_criteria: Any candidate uses new dates, new paid data, new OI files, or strategy PnL selection.
Any candidate hides opposite-right ITM rows.
Any candidate claims true dealer net gamma from OI-only proxy data.
Any candidate upgrades H-G1 to strategy-ready.
next_safe_action: Run a no-paid policy-comparison diagnostic using this pre-registration and manifest-v3 rows only.
